From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

American actor

| Milton Selzer | |
| --- | --- |

| Died | October 21, 2006(2006-10-21) (aged 87)  [Oxnard, California](/wiki/Oxnard,_California "Oxnard, California"), U.S. |

**Milton Selzer** (October 25, 1918 – October 21, 2006) was an American stage, film, and television [actor](/wiki/Actor "Actor").

## Early life

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milton_Selzer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life")]

Born in [Lowell, Massachusetts](/wiki/Lowell,_Massachusetts "Lowell, Massachusetts"), Selzer and his family moved to [Portsmouth, New Hampshire](/wiki/Portsmouth,_New_Hampshire "Portsmouth, New Hampshire"), where he was raised. After graduating from [Portsmouth High School](/wiki/Portsmouth_High_School_(New_Hampshire) "Portsmouth High School (New Hampshire)"), he attended the [University of New Hampshire](/wiki/University_of_New_Hampshire "University of New Hampshire") before serving in [World War II](/wiki/World_War_II "World War II").[[1]](#cite_note-opa-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milton_Selzer&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

Selzer's acting career began with small parts on [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre"). After moving to Hollywood in 1960, he began a prolific career as a character actor making many guest appearances in film and television.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

### Stage

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milton_Selzer&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Stage")]

Selzer's [Broadway](/wiki/Broadway_theatre "Broadway theatre") credits include *Tiger at the Gates* (1955), *Once Upon a Tailor* (1954), *Arms and the Man* (1950), and *Julius Caesar* (1950).[[3]](#cite_note-3)

### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milton_Selzer&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Television")]

Selzer's many television roles included appearances on *[The Twilight Zone](/wiki/The_Twilight_Zone_(1959_TV_series) "The Twilight Zone (1959 TV series)")*, where he portrayed an alien in "[Hocus-Pocus and Frisby](/wiki/Hocus-Pocus_and_Frisby "Hocus-Pocus and Frisby")", and as the miserly son-in-law in "[The Masks](/wiki/The_Masks "The Masks")". He appeared as Dr. Nobel in an episode of *[Have Gun Will Travel](/wiki/Have_Gun_Will_Travel "Have Gun Will Travel")*, He appeared in an episode of *[The Asphalt Jungle](/wiki/The_Asphalt_Jungle_(TV_series) "The Asphalt Jungle (TV series)")* in 1961. He made two memorable guest appearances on *[Perry Mason](/wiki/Perry_Mason_(1957_TV_series) "Perry Mason (1957 TV series)")*; in 1963 he played defendant and title character Dr. Aaron Stuart in "The Case of the Decadent Dean," and in 1964 he played murder victim Dr. Max Taylor in "The Case of the Bullied Bowler".[[1]](#cite_note-opa-1)

From 1965–67, he made seven guest appearances as Professor Parker in *[Get Smart](/wiki/Get_Smart "Get Smart")* followed by roles on *[That Girl](/wiki/That_Girl "That Girl")*, *[The Rat Patrol](/wiki/The_Rat_Patrol "The Rat Patrol")*, *[Hogan's Heroes](/wiki/Hogan%27s_Heroes "Hogan's Heroes")*, and *[The Bold Ones: The Protectors](/wiki/The_Bold_Ones:_The_Protectors "The Bold Ones: The Protectors")*. In the many crime shows of the 1960s and 1970s, Selzer would often portray unsympathetic characters who would also possess sad and often pathetic qualities. He was a regular on the 1973 [situation comedy](/wiki/Situation_comedy "Situation comedy") *[Needles and Pins](/wiki/Needles_and_Pins_(TV_series) "Needles and Pins (TV series)")* as Julius Singer.[[1]](#cite_note-opa-1)

He made his last onscreen appearance in the 1995 [television film](/wiki/Television_film "Television film") *Cagney & Lacey: Together Again*.[[1]](#cite_note-opa-1)

### Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milton_Selzer&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Film")]

Selzer's film career includes roles in *[The Young Savages](/wiki/The_Young_Savages "The Young Savages")* (1961) opposite [Burt Lancaster](/wiki/Burt_Lancaster "Burt Lancaster"), *[A Big Hand for the Little Lady](/wiki/A_Big_Hand_for_the_Little_Lady "A Big Hand for the Little Lady")* (1966), *[In Enemy Country](/wiki/In_Enemy_Country "In Enemy Country")* (1968), *[The Buddy System](/wiki/The_Buddy_System_(film) "The Buddy System (film)")* (1984) and *[Sid and Nancy](/wiki/Sid_and_Nancy "Sid and Nancy")* (1986).[[1]](#cite_note-opa-1)

## Death

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Milton_Selzer&action=edit&section=6 "Edit section: Death")]

On October 21, 2006, four days before his 88th birthday, Selzer died of pulmonary complications.[[1]](#cite_note-opa-1)
